description: Cost, performance, or capability figures stated in present tense when they are projections or targets, not measured results
example: 88% cost reduction stated as achieved when it is a target from UCLA deployment proposal (F-TEXT-004)

description: Design goals or aspirational properties stated as established present-tense facts without qualifying language
example: 'same code, same API, zero rewrites' stated as fact when Docker Desktop vs Kubernetes have different constraints (F-TEXT-006)

description: Validation evidence from N=1 or narrow scope generalized to broad claims without qualifying the evidence boundary
example: '3 validated patterns' when only Pattern 3 has validation evidence from one integration (F-TEXT-009)
graduation_note: freq=5 across whitepaper, SiteForge, L2 infrastructure — spans 3 distinct review domains

description: Descriptive labels or naming conventions presented as enforced protocol-level properties
example: Trust levels (full/verified/limited) are SSH-alias labels, not protocol-enforced access controls (F-TEXT-017)

description: Statistics that anchor a value proposition or design decision cited without attribution or source
example: '73% of enterprise AI projects fail to reach production' — widely cited but unverified here (F-TEXT-003)

description: Design decision documents that omit genuinely different alternatives, listing only variations of the chosen approach
example: ADR about composition patterns lists 3 variations but no alternative architectures like event-driven or pub-sub (F-TEXT-011)

description: Performance multipliers or speed claims stated without specifying the benchmark, model size, task, or baseline
example: 'up to 300x faster performance' without specifying benchmark, model size, or task (F-TEXT-005); M06 dispatch 9.1s warm-cache only, cold-start 39-69s not measured (M11-D-005)

description: Claiming industry consensus or standard validation based on 2-3 sources that describe similar patterns but don't constitute consensus
example: 'validated by industry consensus' based on Deloitte + Ericsson — two sources describing similar models (F-TEXT-008)

description: Documents that derive from a live authoritative source but lack synchronization dates or freshness indicators; also: summary documents that present derived state as verified live state
example: Lattice Network Alpha updated 12 days ago (F-TEXT-016); VC5 report claims Tailscale ACL 'active' based on policy doc with status drafted_not_applied (M11-S-001)
graduation_note: freq=5 across whitepaper and L2 infrastructure; systematically present in infrastructure-domain III reviews

description: Forward-looking mappings between current and proposed concepts presented without conjectural or speculative markers
example: Federation Readiness Mapping presents current-to-future field mappings as established, none of the federation equivalents exist yet (F-TEXT-019)

description: Related but distinct concept lists presented without explicit differentiation, leaving the reader to infer how they relate
example: Five Traps (failure mode categories) and 5 INSPECT checks (operational questions) overlap on confidence/completeness but serve different purposes — no clarifying note (F-TEXT-004)

description: A single brand color value used as both foreground text and background fill across light/dark modes, causing WCAG AA contrast failures in one mode
example: WGA brand green #2D8659 used as hero text (dark mode) and card background (light mode) — failed AA contrast in both contexts until split into --brand-primary (mode-aware) and --brand-bg (stable)

description: Interactive container elements (div[role='button'], button) wrapping other interactive children (links, buttons), creating accessibility violations and ambiguous click targets
example: WGA PersonaSelector used div[role='button'] containing <a> links — axe-core flagged nested-interactive. Fix: restructured to radio group pattern

description: Generated pages lack JSON-LD structured data despite it being a spec requirement and quality gate criterion, discovered only when Playwright meta gate runs
example: WGA site had JSON-LD on 2/8 pages — M10 Gate 6 caught it. All 8 pages needed Organization/WebPage/CollectionPage structured data

description: Entity-specific names, taglines, navigation items, and metadata hardcoded directly in components rather than sourced from branding config or content collections
example: WGA: 14 hardcoded entity references across 8 files (Header navItems, Footer brand heading, SEOHead siteName, BaseLayout ogImage, PersonaSelector personas, index stats/programs)

description: Data that should live in content collections or YAML config files hardcoded as JavaScript const arrays in page components, breaking the content/code separation pattern
example: WGA curriculum modules defined as const array in index.astro instead of using the programs content collection that already has a helper function (collections.ts:10-15)

description: An analogy established for explanatory purposes is later used for competitive positioning or structural importance claims, exceeding its original bounded scope
example: DNS analogy bounded as 'architectural, not mechanistic' in Section 1.2 but used for competitive positioning in Section 8.7: 'HuggingFace Hub is to the Lattice Marketplace as a proprietary DNS provider is to the DNS protocol itself' (F-TEXT-003)

description: Body text appropriately hedges claims (RFC-style markers, conjectural labels, phase disclaimers) but summary sections (abstract, exec summary, conclusion) strip these hedges and present everything in present-tense declarative prose
example: Section 5.5 marks economic features as Phase 1 = disabled, but exec summary states economic layer 'sustains protocol operations' in present tense (F-TEXT-002); DG1 'GO-RECOMMENDED' headline doesn't surface FLAG #17; M06 'E2E proved' doesn't surface HTTP-bypass deviation (M11 meta-pattern MP-1); M13 INTROSPECT Check 3/5: VC2 'federation dispatch works' claim doesn't surface HTTP path broken (FU-07 TypeError) or n=1 warm-cache-only scope

description: Different semantic interpretations of the supremum operation across domains (conservative vs evidential) presented without distinction, causing readers to assume uniform behavior
example: Compliance supremum tightens requirements (conservative), data quality supremum elevates attestation (evidential) — both called 'supremum' without noting the semantic direction difference (F-TEXT-009)

description: A document intended as a friendly peer-to-peer note to the authors drifts into reviewer-stance headers or phrasing (e.g. section titled 'Review', imperative bullets, prescriptive 'Lead with X'), eroding the collaborator tone
example: Partner-shareable note to VaaS paper authors titled Section A 'ML/AI-Perspective Review' and used imperative bullets in Section B.2 ('Lead with...', 'Architecture figure on page 1.') despite author brief of friendly-peer register (F-TONE-004, F-PEER-003)

description: A document meant as a playful fun-case-study share carries residual proposal architecture from an internal charter/scoping pass — parallel slot-labelled subsections (What:/Who:), prerequisite checklists, 'concept sketch' framing — which reads as a formal proposal rather than a floated idea
example: MG concept note titled 'A Short Concept Sketch', with three parallel ### A [role] partner subsections each shaped as What:/Who:, and a 6-bullet 'light list of things to talk through' checklist — all flagged 3+ voices (F-TONE-002, F-TONE-003, F-CLIN-001, F-CLIN-002)

description: The same hedging formula is restated 3+ times in a single short document (opener + mid + close), cumulatively reading as over-apologetic and eroding the confidence the hedge is meant to protect. Loosely related to C-011 but targeted at hedging language rather than concept lists
example: MG note repeated 'no ask / no commitment / no expectation' triple at opener, mid-doc, and close in an 800-word document (F-COLD-003)

description: A document opens with a warm first-name salutation ('Dear Ankit, Milit, ...') but signs off anonymously ('a small team in adjacent contexts'), creating register tension between intimate opening and distant closing
example: Partner-shareable ML note opened 'Dear Ankit, Milit, Anna, Maarten, Wesley, Stephen' but signed '— With thanks and collegiality, a small team working on verification pipelines in adjacent contexts' (F-COLD-001)

description: A security risk is formally accepted as mitigated by a named control (ACL, firewall rule, protocol restriction) whose live state has not been verified — the acceptance argument is structurally valid but the premise is unconfirmed
example: SSH PasswordAuthentication yes accepted as safe because 'Tailscale ACL restricts access' — but the ACL was drafted_not_applied at time of acceptance (M11-S-002)

description: A follow-up item requiring a human action (password backup, key rotation, physical procedure) is registered as non-blocking FU with no verification artifact, accountability loop, or due-date mechanism — the action may never be completed
example: node_key.pem backup to password manager registered as FU-05 'human action, unconfirmed' in M05 with no verification evidence across 7 subsequent missions (M11-D-001)

description: Two or more bugs in the same execution path that prevent each other from being individually observed by testing the endpoint. Fixing one exposes the other. The path always fails (for different reasons at different depths), masking both bugs from standard integration testing. Only discoverable via code-path reading.
example: FU-07 TypeError in FederationRouter() call + FU-05/12 auth gate both blocking POST /jobs/federated: auth gate fires first (401), masking the TypeError that fires second if auth is bypassed. Neither discovered by VC testing because the Python in-process dispatch path bypassed both. Discovered in M13 III cycle via code review (L2 Genesis).

description: Producer module emits structured field that no consumer aggregator yet reads; field exists per spec but downstream wiring lands in a later phase
example: VideoForge M_3_02 RegisterCoherenceScorer emits RegisterCoherenceResult.requires_human_approval + r11_metadata before Critic-aggregator / LangGraph HITL wires it (Phase 4 M_4_01 + Phase 5 M_5_03 deliverables). Recurred in M_3_03 (HookStrengthScorer.candidates_by_platform + visual_dependency) and M_3_04 (EngagementScorer.predicted_engagement_* + engagement_model_version). Frequency 3 across 3 sessions per ADR-003 §3

description: Vault-side substrate (specs/ADRs/pattern docs) is ported into code as canonical runtime source-of-truth via copy-faithful constants; risks substrate drift if spec amends without parallel code update; mitigation = Standing Rule 12 dual-filing + drift-check on spec amendment
example: VideoForge M_3_02 register_definitions.py ports R1-R12 essences + 5 anti-blend rules from spec_register_compliance.md §4 + p2_voice_register_pattern.md. M_3_03 hook_definitions.py ports T1-T11 templates + PLATFORM_HOOK_WEIGHT_MATRIX 7×11 from spec_hook_detection.md §§2/4/5. M_3_04 heuristic_rules.py ports 13 doctrine rules + thresholds + register index map from spec_engagement_prediction.md §8.1 + retention_diagnostics.md + dimensions_of_iteration.md §14 + sota_scan §2 + platform_targeting_matrix.md + quality_gates.md
